creator: ngie@FreeBSD.org
text:
FYI BTW..
-Garrett

On Wed, Jul 27, 2011 at 6:50 PM, Garrett Cooper <yaneurabeya@gmail.com> wrote:
> On Wed, Jul 27, 2011 at 1:18 PM,  <sylvio@freebsd.org> wrote:
>> Synopsis: [patch] Upgrade net-mgmt/net-snmp to 5.7 // resolve build issues on CURRENT
>>
>> State-Changed-From-To: open->closed
>> State-Changed-By: sylvio
>> State-Changed-When: Wed Jul 27 20:18:58 UTC 2011
>> State-Changed-Why:
>> Committed, with minor changes. Thanks!
>
>    Thanks for the help Sylvio!
>    BTW -- this patch will probably reduce unnecessary complaints from users:
>
> fetch -o - 'http://net-snmp.git.sourceforge.net/git/gitweb.cgi?p=net-snmp/net-snmp;a=commitdiff_plain;h=2edccc9be064dc4d669f31d4dc0c571f648ad34d;hp=994baa9f2032815a46b0f812149e6ab82aa8d381'
> | sed -E -e 's,[ab]/,,g' > files/patch-snmplib-parse.c
>
>    I'm going to commit it with the local version of our copy of
> net-snmp at least.

   I missed the following patches in the PR (they were in my local
workspace), so the build on 9-CURRENT is still broken -_-...
